task_completion.visual_evidence: A Jupyter Notebook interface (browser or IDE) is visible with the filename development_feasibility.ipynb.

task_completion.description: Code calculates FAR and filters for feasible parcels
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code cells show reading sanfran_public.h5, converting shape_area from square meters to square feet, calculating current FAR (building sqft / parcel area), and filtering for parcels where current FAR is less than 50% of max_far.

task_completion.description: Summary of total and feasible parcels is outputted
task_completion.visual_evidence: A notebook cell output displays a printed summary showing the total number of parcels and the number of feasible parcels.

task_completion.description: CSV of top 100 feasible parcels is generated and saved
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code shows sorting by unused capacity ratio, selecting the top 100 rows, keeping columns 'parcel_id', 'current_density', 'max_allowed', and 'unused_capacity_ratio', and saving to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/feasible_parcels.csv.

task_completion.description: Bar chart of unused capacity ratio distribution is generated and saved
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code shows generating a bar chart of the unused capacity ratio distribution and saving it to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/feasibility_distribution.png, or the plot is visible in the notebook output/file viewer.

integrity.visual_evidence: Execution counters (e.g., In [1]:, Out [1]:) are visible next to the code cells, indicating the code was actually run in the notebook.

integrity.description: The agent wrote actual code to perform the calculations rather than hardcoding results
integrity.visual_evidence: The code contains pandas/math operations for the FAR calculation and filtering, rather than creating a dummy dataframe or hardcoding summary numbers.

integrity.description: The output files are generated by the code
integrity.visual_evidence: The paths for the CSV and PNG files in the code exactly match the required output paths, and there is no evidence of the agent downloading or copying pre-made files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must use the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/development_feasibility.ipynb and data from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5. The output CSV must be saved to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/feasible_parcels.csv and contain exactly 100 rows (top 100 parcels) with columns: parcel_id, current_density, max_allowed, unused_capacity_ratio. The output plot must be saved to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/feasibility_distribution.png.
